title: श्री अय्यप्पा स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री अय्यप्पा स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ महाशास्त्रे नमस्तुभ्यं शरणं देवि पालय।
भूतनाथाय विद्महे भवनाशाय धीमहि।
तन्नः शास्ता प्रचोदयात्॥

विष्णुशम्भोः सुतं देवं शास्तारं प्रणमाम्यहम्।
हरिहरपुत्रं शास्तारं वन्दे सबरिगिरीश्वरम्॥१॥

मणिकण्ठं महावीरं महाशास्त्रं महेश्वरम्।
पम्पातीरनिवासं च शबरीशं नमाम्यहम्॥२॥

पुलिकेशीगजारूढं पुष्कलेश्वरसेवितम्।
पूर्णापुष्कलानाथं शबरीशं नमाम्यहम्॥३॥

श्रीभूतनाथं वरदं भक्तानामभयप्रदम्।
भवरोगहरं वैद्यं शबरीशं नमाम्यहम्॥४॥

त्यागमूर्तिं दयासिन्धुं ब्रह्मचर्यव्रतस्थितम्।
नैष्ठिकब्रह्मचारिणं शबरीशं नमाम्यहम्॥५॥

पन्नगाभरणं देवं अनाथानां परायणम्।
पन्तलराजकुमारं च शबरीशं नमाम्यहम्॥६॥

महिषीदानवध्वंसिनं भक्तरक्षणतत्परम्।
पम्पाविलासमूर्तिं च शबरीशं नमाम्यहम्॥७॥

धर्मशास्तारमीशानं ज्योतिर्मयमनामयम्।
त्रैलोक्यपूजितं देवं शबरीशं नमाम्यहम्॥८॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
य इदं पठति नित्यं श्रीशास्तुः स्तोत्रमुत्तमम्।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति॥

स्वामिये शरणमय्यप्पा॥

॥ इति श्री अय्यप्पा स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ayyappa Stotram ||

Om Mahaashaastre Namastubhyam Sharanam Devi Paalaya.
Bhootanaathaaya Vidmahe Bhavanaa​shaaya Dheemahi.
Tannah Shaastaa Prachodayaat.

VishnuShambhoh Sutam Devam Shastaaram Pranamaamyaham.
Hariharaputram Shastaaram Vande Sabarigiree​shvaram.||1||

Manikantham Mahaaveeram Mahaashaastram Maheshvaram.
Pampaateeranivaasam Cha Shabareesham Namaamyaham.||2||

Pulikesheegajaaroo dham Pushkale​shvarasevitam.
Poornaa​pushkala​naatham Shabareesham Namaamyaham.||3||

Shreebhootanaatham Varadam Bhaktaanaamabhayapradam.
Bhavarogaharam Vaidyam Shabareesham Namaamyaham.||4||

Tyaagamoortim Dayaasindhum Brahma​charya​vrata​sthitam.
Naishthi​ka​brahmachaarinam Shabareesham Namaamyaham.||5||

Pannagaabharanam Devam Anaathaanaam Parayanam.
Pantalaraajkumaaram Cha Shabareesham Namaamyaham.||6||

Mahishee Daanavadhvansinam Bhaktarakshana​tatparam.
Pampaavilasamoortim Cha Shabareesham Namaamyaham.||7||

Dharmashaastaaram​eeshaanam Jyotirmayam​anaamayam.
Trailokyapoojitam Devam Shabareesham Namaamyaham.||8||

|| Phalashruti ||
Ya Idam Pathati Nityam Shreeshaastuh Stotramuttamam.
Sarvapaapa vinirmuktah Vishnulokam Sa Gachchhati.

Swamiye Sharanmayyappa.

|| Iti Shri Ayyappa Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
ॐ महाशास्त्रे नमस्कार! शरण में रक्षा करो। भूतनाथ को जानते हैं, भवनाश के लिए ध्यान करते हैं, वे शास्ता हमें प्रेरित करें।

(1) विष्णु और शम्भु के पुत्र, शास्ता देव को प्रणाम करता हूँ। हरिहरपुत्र शास्ता, सबरिगिरीश्वर को वन्दन।

(2) मणिकण्ठ (गले में मणि धारण करने वाले), महावीर, महाशास्त्र, महेश्वर, पम्पा तीर पर निवास करने वाले शबरीश को नमस्कार।

(3) व्याघ्रारूढ (पुलि-बाघ पर सवार), पुष्कलेश्वर द्वारा सेवित, पूर्णा और पुष्कला के नाथ शबरीश को नमस्कार।

(4) भूतनाथ, वरदान देने वाले, भक्तों को अभय प्रदान करने वाले, भवरोग (संसार-रोग) हरने वाले वैद्य, शबरीश को नमस्कार।

(5) त्याग की मूर्ति, दया के सिन्धु, ब्रह्मचर्य व्रत में स्थित, नैष्ठिक ब्रह्मचारी शबरीश को नमस्कार।

(6) सर्पाभूषण धारी देव, अनाथों के परायण, पन्तल (पन्डालम) के राजकुमार शबरीश को नमस्कार।

(7) महिषी दानवी का ध्वंस करने वाले, भक्तरक्षण में तत्पर, पम्पा के विलास-मूर्ति शबरीश को नमस्कार।

(8) धर्मशास्ता, ईशान, ज्योतिर्मय, अनामय (रोगरहित), त्रैलोक्य द्वारा पूजित देव शबरीश को नमस्कार।

जो इस उत्तम शास्ता-स्तोत्र को नित्य पढ़ता है, वह सर्वपापों से मुक्त होकर विष्णुलोक को जाता है। स्वामिये शरणमय्यप्पा!
benefits: Sacred prayer to Lord Ayyappa, the son of Hari (Vishnu) and Hara (Shiva)
Invokes Ayyappa as Dharma Shasta - the supreme teacher of righteousness
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of diseases, fears, and sins from devotees' lives
Essential for Sabarimala pilgrimage and 41-day Mandala Kalam vrat
Invokes fearlessness and protection from all dangers
Invokes the blessing of liberation and passage to vishnu-loka
description: Dedicated to Lord Ayyappa, the Ayyappa Stotram (श्री अय्यप्पा स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. Attributed to Traditional Sanskrit Hymn, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to remove all diseases, fears, and sins from devotees' lives, invoke Ayyappa as Dharma Shasta - the supreme teacher of righteousness, and grant fearlessness and protection from all dangers. For best results, devotees chant this on Saturday (Shanivar) and during Makara Vilakku and Mandala Puja. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 5 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Ayyappa Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Ayyappa Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn to Lord Ayyappa of Sabarimala - Hariharaputra, Dharma Shasta, Shabareesh.
relatedSearches: Ayyappa Stotram lyrics in Sanskrit Malayalam Tamil PDF
Hariharaputra story Mohini Shiva birth of Ayyappa

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ayyappa Stotram and which deity does it praise?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ayyappa Stotram is a Sanskrit devotional hymn praising Lord Ayyappa, the presiding deity of Sabarimala in Kerala. The hymn opens with the Shasta Gayatri (Bhutanathaya vidmahe) and proceeds through eight verses each ending in Shabareesham namamy-aham — 'I bow to the Lord of Shabari mountain'. Ayyappa is celebrated here as Hariharaputra (son of Hari-Vishnu and Hara-Shiva), Manikantha (the bell-necked one), Mahashasta (the great teacher), Naishtika Brahmachari (perpetual celibate), the slayer of the demoness Mahishi, and the prince of Pandalam. The stotra closes with the universal Sabarimala chant: Swamiye Sharanam Ayyappa. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi Ayyappa hymns; Sabarimala temple portal; Hindupedia Ayyappa.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Ayyappa and how was he born?
seoFaqs.answer: Ayyappa is the son of Lord Vishnu in his Mohini avatar and Lord Shiva, hence his title Hariharaputra (Hari + Hara + Putra). According to the Brahmanda Purana and Sabarimala sthala-purana, after the churning of the ocean, when Vishnu took the Mohini form to distribute amrita, Shiva fell in love with that form and from their union was born Ayyappa. He was found as an infant on the banks of the Pampa river by King Rajashekhara of Pandalam, who raised him as Manikantha (because of the bell on his neck). Ayyappa eventually slew the buffalo-demoness Mahishi and ascended Sabari mountain to take eternal residence as the Yogi-deity. (Sources: Brahmanda Purana; Sabarimala portal; Wikipedia Ayyappa.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Ayyappa worshipped as a Naishtika Brahmachari?
seoFaqs.answer: The fifth verse of the stotra praises Ayyappa as Tyaga-murti, Daya-sindhu, Brahmacharya-vrata-sthita, Naishtika-Brahmachari — the embodiment of renunciation, ocean of compassion, fixed in the vow of celibacy, the perpetual celibate. Unlike most Hindu deities who have consorts, Ayyappa is a yogi-deva who took an eternal vow of brahmacharya. This is the spiritual reason why women of menstruating age (10-50) traditionally do not climb to the Sabarimala sannidhanam — the deity's vow is honoured by the devotee community. The 41-day Mandala Vratam observed by male pilgrims mirrors this naishtika brahmacharya. (Sources: Sabarimala Devaswom Board; Hindupedia Ayyappa; Sabarimala sthala-purana.)

seoFaqs.question: When should Ayyappa Stotram be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Saturday (Shanivar) is the day specially sacred to Ayyappa, since he is regarded as Shanaishchara-priya. The supreme occasion is the 41-day Mandala Vratam from mid-November to late December, culminating in Mandala Puja on the eleventh day of Vrishchika month, and Makara Vilakku on Makara Sankranti (mid-January) when the celestial Makara Jyoti appears at Ponnambalamedu. Devotees observing the vrata recite the stotra after dawn bath, before the irumudi-kettu, and during the climb of the eighteen sacred steps (pathinettu padi). It is also chanted on Vishu (Kerala new year) and during the temple's annual Painkuni festival. (Sources: Sabarimala Devaswom Board calendar; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of 'Swamiye Sharanam Ayyappa' chanted at the end?
seoFaqs.answer: Swamiye Sharanam Ayyappa is the Malayalam-Sanskrit shara-naagati mantra meaning 'O Lord, I take refuge in you, Ayyappa'. It is the universal salutation of the Sabarimala pilgrim and is repeated continuously during the entire 41-day vrata, during the trek through the forest, while climbing the 18 steps, and at darshan. The stotra closes with this chant to invoke total surrender to Ayyappa. The phrase encapsulates the bhakta-bhagavan relationship: Swami (master) + iye (vocative) + Sharanam (refuge) + Ayyappa (the deity). Devotees address each other as Ayyappa or Swami during the vrata to dissolve all social distinctions. (Sources: Sabarimala portal; Travancore Devaswom Board guides.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Ayyappa Stotram grant according to its phalashruti?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti declares that whoever recites this excellent Shasta-stotra daily becomes free from all sins (sarva-papa-vinirmukta) and attains Vishnu-loka (Vishnulokam sa gachchhati). The hymn promises removal of fear of disease (the fourth verse calls Ayyappa Bhava-roga-haram Vaidyam — the physician who cures the disease of samsara), abhaya (fearlessness) for devotees, victory over inner demons symbolised by Mahishi, success in 41-day vrata observance, safe forest passage to Sabarimala, and ultimate liberation. Tradition holds that recitation during the Mandala Vratam multiplies its merit a thousandfold. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi phalashruti; Sabarimala temple guides.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Ayyappa Stotram differ from Ayyappa Ashtakam and Ayyappa Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: All three hymns belong to the Ayyappa devotional corpus but vary in length and purpose. The Ayyappa Stotram (8 verses) is the most popular medium-length praise focused on iconographic salutation ending each verse with Shabareesham namamy-aham. The Ayyappa Ashtakam is a shorter eight-stanza meditation with a tighter metric structure, used for quick daily japa. The Ayyappa Sahasranama Stotram lists 1000 names of the deity and is recited on Saturdays and during Mandala Vratam by serious upasakas — it takes about 30-40 minutes versus 5 minutes for the stotram. Most Sabarimala pilgrims chant the stotram daily and the sahasranama on special days. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi Ayyappa corpus; Sabarimala Devaswom guides.)
